description: The sign() method passes the full message to tss_esapi MaxBuffer for TPM-side hashing (secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs:312). TPM 2.0 MaxBuffer is limited to TPM2_MAX_DIGEST_BUFFER (typically 1024 bytes). Messages exceeding this limit fail with 'message too large for TPM hash' — a confusing error for callers who do not know they are hitting a TPM-specific limit. The trait-level sign() contract (secretx-core/src/lib.rs:1398-1409) does not mention any message size limit. The PKCS#11 backend has no such limit because CKM_ECDSA_SHA256 handles hashing internally. Document the precondition on the TPM2 impl and consider pre-hashing the message on the host side (pass the SHA-256 digest to ctx.sign directly) to remove the size limit entirely, matching the behavior of other backends.
title: tpm2: sign() has undocumented message size limit (~1024 bytes)
